title: stylidium elachophyllum
intro: Stylidium elachophyllum, commonly known as the prickly triggerplant, is a unique and fascinating plant species that belongs to the Stylidiaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Stylidium elachophyllum include its potential to alleviate skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using a decoction of the roots and stems to create a topical treatment.
